reasoning:
On June 5, 2025, the European Central Bank (ECB) announced a 25 basis point reduction in its key interest rates, lowering the deposit facility rate from 2.25% to 2.00%. This change explicitly takes effect on the start of the next maintenance period on June 11, 2025. The Euro Short-Term Rate (€STR) tracks the deposit facility rate very closely (usually trading about 8 to 10 basis points below it). Therefore, the €STR will structurally drop by 25 basis points from its current pre-cut level of ~2.17% down to approximately 1.92% starting June 11. 

Because the benchmark date in the question is June 8, 2025 (before the cut takes effect), the baseline rate for comparison will be around 2.17%. For all subsequent resolution dates (June 15, July 8, September 6, and December 5), the €STR will operate under the new, lower ECB rate regime. Forecasters and market participants widely expect the ECB to maintain this rate or deliver further cuts (with projections pointing to a 1.5% deposit rate by year-end 2025) as inflation remains below target and economic growth is sluggish. The probability of the ECB executing a dramatic reversal to hike rates back above 2.25% by December is extremely low. Thus, the €STR is effectively guaranteed to be well below the June 8 benchmark on all resolution dates, making an increase highly unlikely.
